label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to find the remainder when 3^200 is divided by 13. Hmm, that's a pretty big exponent. I remember something about modular arithmetic and maybe Fermat's Little Theorem? Let me think. First, Fermat's Little Theorem says that if p is a prime number, then for any integer a not divisible by p, a^(p-1) ≡ 1 mod p. Since 13 is a prime number and 3 isn't divisible by 13, this theorem should apply here. So, according to that, 3^(12) ≡ 1 mod 13. So, if 3^12 leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 13, then maybe I can break
